conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by reading the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now let me help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have two children - Alex, who's 12, and Blake, who's 8.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have two children - Alex (12) and Blake (8).

Now I need to ask you some questions to assess your eligibility for each child.

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your relationship with each child.

**Do both Alex and Blake live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question to complete my assessment:

**Are either Alex or Blake currently in hospital for more than 12 weeks, or in care for more than 8 weeks?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are in hospital or in care.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.

Based on what you've told me:
- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- You have two children: Alex (12) and Blake (8)
- Both children live with you
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them
- Neither child is in hospital or care

According to the official guidance, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You're usually considered responsible for a child if you live with them.

Since both Alex (12) and Blake (8) are under 16, live with you in the UK, and no one else is claiming for them, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for both children.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

You meet all the requirements:
- ✓ You live in the UK
- ✓ Both children are under 16
- ✓ You're responsible for them (they live with you)
- ✓ No one else is claiming for them
- ✓ Neither child is in hospital or care

You can claim Child Benefit for both children. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 12 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Blake is 8 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for them. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and Blake is not in hospital or care. Therefore, the claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with two children, Alex (12) and Blake (8), both under 16 years of age. The claimant is responsible for both children as they live together. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child, and neither child is in hospital or care. The claimant meets all eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for both children.
